name: Polka-Dot Brownies
recipeIngredient: 3 ounces semisweet chocolate (coarsely chopped) or 3 ounces semisweet chocolate chips
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Lightly spray an 8-inch square baking pan with nonstick cooking spray.
3. In the top of a double boiler, over barely simmering water (or in microwave), melt chocolate and shortening; remove from heat and stir until smooth. Set aside to cool to room temperature.
4. In a large bowl, beat eggs and sugar at high speed with an electric mixer until pale yellow, thick, and tripled in volume, about 7 minutes.
5. Beat in vanilla and salt. Stir in cooled chocolate mixture by hand, then fold in flour just until incorporated.
6. Pour batter evenly into prepared pan and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until a knife inserted in the center comes out almost clean. If brownies are slightly underbaked, they will have a fudgier texture.
7. Cool on a rack before cutting into 8 equal bars. Dust with confectioners' sugar or cocoa if desired. Makes 8 servings of 1 brownie each.
